name: DatePicker.DateInput

unions.name: DateInput
unions.comment: The opaque type representing a particular DateInput instance.

unions.name: InputError
unions.comment: Possible errors in the DateInput field.

unions.name: Msg
unions.comment: Messages that can be sent through user interaction to update the DateInput model.

aliases.name: Config
aliases.type: { dateInputSettings : DatePicker.DateInput.Settings, isDayDisabled : Time.Zone -> Time.Posix -> Basics.Bool, zone : Time.Zone, theme : DatePicker.Theme.Theme, id : String.String }

aliases.name: DateFormat
aliases.type: { pattern : DatePicker.DateInput.DatePattern, separator : Char.Char, placeholders : DatePicker.DateInput.DateParts Char.Char }

aliases.name: TimeFormat
aliases.type: { separator : Char.Char, placeholders : DatePicker.DateInput.TimeParts Char.Char, allowedTimesOfDay : Time.Zone -> Time.Posix -> { startHour : Basics.Int, startMinute : Basics.Int, endHour : Basics.Int, endMinute : Basics.Int } }

values.name: catchError
values.comment:
Checks if the input value, date parts, and time parts are valid
and allowed based on the provided configuration, returning an optional error.
values.type: DatePicker.DateInput.Config -> String.String -> DatePicker.DateInput.DateParts (Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int) -> DatePicker.DateInput.TimeParts (Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int) -> Maybe.Maybe DatePicker.DateInput.InputError

values.name: clear
values.comment: Clears the DateInput field.

values.name: containerId
values.comment: Generates the ID for the container element based on the provided configuration.

values.name: defaultConfig
values.comment: Default configuration for DateInput.

values.name: defaultDateFormat
values.comment: Default date format pattern.

values.name: defaultFormat
values.comment: Default format used for DateInput.

values.name: defaultSettings
values.comment: Default settings for DateInput.

values.name: defaultTimeFormat
values.comment: Default time format settings.

values.name: durationEndId
values.comment: Generates the ID for a duration's end input element based on the provided configuration.

values.name: durationStartId
values.comment: Generates the ID for a duration's start input element based on the provided configuration.

values.name: hasDurationError
values.comment: Checks if one of the two DateInputs of a duration input has an error, returning a boolean and an optional error.
values.type: Time.Zone -> ( DatePicker.DateInput.DateInput msg, DatePicker.DateInput.DateInput msg ) -> ( Basics.Bool, Maybe.Maybe DatePicker.DateInput.InputError )

values.name: hasError
values.comment: Checks if the DateInput has an error.

values.name: init
values.comment: Returns a DateInput instance initialized with default values.

values.name: inputValueToParts
values.comment: Converts an input string into date and time parts.
values.type: DatePicker.DateInput.Format -> String.String -> ( DatePicker.DateInput.DateParts (Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int), DatePicker.DateInput.TimeParts (Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int) )

values.name: isInitial
values.comment: Checks if it's still the initial model

values.name: partsToInputValue
values.comment: Converts date and time parts into an input string value.
values.type: DatePicker.DateInput.Format -> DatePicker.DateInput.DateParts (Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int) -> DatePicker.DateInput.TimeParts (Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int) -> Maybe.Maybe String.String

values.name: partsToPosix
values.comment:
Converts date and time parts into a Posix time.

Returns Nothing if the parts do not form a valid date/time.
values.type: Time.Zone -> DatePicker.DateInput.DateParts (Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int) -> DatePicker.DateInput.TimeParts (Maybe.Maybe Basics.Int) -> Maybe.Maybe Time.Posix

values.name: sanitizeInputValue
values.comment: Cleans and truncates the user input value based on the given format.

values.name: textFieldId
values.comment: Generates the ID for the text field element based on the provided configuration.

values.name: toPosix
values.comment: Converts a DateInput model into a Posix time, if possible.

values.name: update
values.comment:
Update the DateInput based on user interaction according to the given internal msg.

Returns the updated DateInput and a Cmd msg.
values.type: DatePicker.DateInput.Config -> DatePicker.DateInput.Msg -> DatePicker.DateInput.DateInput msg -> ( DatePicker.DateInput.DateInput msg, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg )

values.name: updateFromPosix
values.comment:
Updates the DateInput model from a given Posix time.

Converts the time into date and time parts and updates the model accordingly.
values.type: DatePicker.DateInput.Config -> Time.Zone -> Time.Posix -> DatePicker.DateInput.DateInput msg -> DatePicker.DateInput.DateInput msg

values.name: view
values.comment: Renders a single date input element view with error handling and theme styling.
values.type: List.List (Html.Styled.Attribute msg) -> DatePicker.DateInput.Config -> DatePicker.DateInput.DateInput msg -> Html.Styled.Html msg

values.name: viewContainer
values.comment:
Renders a container element for the date input element.
This element is scaled to the date input element's size and is used to position the picker popover.
values.type: DatePicker.Theme.Theme -> List.List (Html.Styled.Attribute msg) -> List.List (Html.Styled.Html msg) -> Html.Styled.Html msg

values.name: viewDurationInputs
values.comment: Renders two date input fields (start and end) for duration picking with error handling and theme styling.
values.type: List.List (Html.Styled.Attribute msg) -> DatePicker.DateInput.Config -> ( DatePicker.DateInput.DateInput msg, DatePicker.DateInput.DateInput msg ) -> Html.Styled.Html msg

values.name: viewPlaceholder
values.comment:
Renders a placeholder view based on the configuration.
This element is empty and scaled to the date input element's size. It is used in combination with
the picker popover working as a placeholder to arrange the picker popover's layout. The real date
input element will be positioned fixed above this element when the picker popover is opened.

name: DatePicker.Settings

unions.name: Preset
unions.comment: A shared type facilitating the preset variants for both pickers

unions.name: TimePickerVisibility
unions.comment:
Set the visibility of the timepicker in the `DateTimePicker`

`NeverVisible` - The time picker is never visible. Please note that
while ostensibly picking a day, a selection still returns a Posix
representing the beginning of that day (00:00). It is up to you to
process the selection accordingly if you wish to treat it as a whole day.

`Toggleable` - The time picker visibility can be toggled but
is by default closed when the datetime picker is opened. Additional
configuration can be achieved via `TimePickerSettings`.

`AlwaysVisible` - The time picker is always visible. This is the default setting
as it most explicitly shows that the datetime picker is indeed picking both
a date and time, not simply a date. Additional configuration can be achieved
via `TimePickerSettings`.

aliases.name: PresetDateConfig
aliases.comment:
Set type facilitating the preset dates
`title` - the displayed name of the preset
`date` - the `Posix` the preset will select in the date picker.

aliases.name: PresetRangeConfig
aliases.comment:
Set type facilitating the preset date ranges
`title` - the displayed name of the preset
`range` - the time range the date range picker will select, consisting of
a start-`Posix` and an end-`Posix`.
aliases.type: { title : String.String, range : { start : Time.Posix, end : Time.Posix } }

aliases.name: Settings
aliases.comment:
The type facilitating the configuration of the datepicker settings.

`id` - provides a custom id to the picker element
`zone` - the `Zone` in which the date picker is being used (client zone)
`formattedDay` - a function that returns a string representation for the provided day of the week
`formattedMonth` - a function that returns a string representation for the provided month
`isDayDisabled` - a function that determines if the combined `Posix` and `Zone` represent a day that should be disabled in the picker
`focusedDate` - a `Posix` that represents a day that should be highlighted on the picker (i.e. the current day)
`dateStringFn` - a function that returns a string representation of the selected day
`timePickerVisibility` - see below
`showCalendarWeekNumbers` - wheather to display or not display caldendar week numbers
`presets` - a list of `Presets`, for selectable, preconfigured dates
`theme` - a record of customizable design tokens

More information can be found in the [examples](https://github.com/mercurymedia/elm-datetime-picker/tree/master/examples).
aliases.type: { zone : Time.Zone, id : String.String, firstWeekDay : Time.Weekday, formattedDay : Time.Weekday -> String.String, formattedMonth : Time.Month -> String.String, isDayDisabled : Time.Zone -> Time.Posix -> Basics.Bool, focusedDate : Maybe.Maybe Time.Posix, dateStringFn : Time.Zone -> Time.Posix -> String.String, timePickerVisibility : DatePicker.Settings.TimePickerVisibility, showCalendarWeekNumbers : Basics.Bool, presets : List.List DatePicker.Settings.Preset, theme : DatePicker.Theme.Theme, dateInputSettings : DatePicker.DateInput.Settings }

aliases.name: TimePickerSettings
aliases.comment:
The type facilitating the configuration of the timepicker settings.

`timeStringFn` - a function that returns a string representation of the selected time of day

Because it could be the case that a picker is being used in a different
timezone than the home timezone of the implementor, the `allowedTimesofDay`
function ingests a `Zone` in addition to a `Posix`. The
`Zone` represents the time zone in which the picker is being used. An
implementor can leverage this to compare against a base time zone when
enforcing allowable times of day, etc. You SHOULD assume that the `Posix`
passed into these functions is floored to the start of its respective `Day`.

More information can be found in the [examples](https://github.com/mercurymedia/elm-datetime-picker/tree/master/examples).
aliases.type: { timeStringFn : Time.Zone -> Time.Posix -> String.String, allowedTimesOfDay : Time.Zone -> Time.Posix -> { startHour : Basics.Int, startMinute : Basics.Int, endHour : Basics.Int, endMinute : Basics.Int } }

values.name: defaultSettings
values.comment:
A record of default settings for the date picker. Extend this if
you want to further customize the date picker.

Requires a `Zone` to inform the picker in which time zone it should
display the selected duration as well as a `msg` that expects a tuple containing
a datepicker instance and a `Maybe` tuple representing a selected duration.

    ( DatePicker, Maybe ( Posix, Posix ) ) -> msg

values.name: defaultTimePickerSettings
values.comment:
A record of default settings for the time picker. Extend this if
you want to further customize the time picker.

values.name: generatePickerDay
values.comment: Transforms a `Posix` into a `PickerDay` based on the `Settings`

values.name: getTimePickerSettings
values.comment: Extracts the `TimePickerSettings` from the `Settings`

values.name: isPresetDateActive
values.comment: Determines if a selected date matches a given preset
values.type: DatePicker.Settings.Settings -> Maybe.Maybe ( DatePicker.Utilities.PickerDay, Time.Posix ) -> DatePicker.Settings.PresetDateConfig -> Basics.Bool

values.name: isPresetRangeActive
values.comment: Determines if a selected date range matches a given preset range
values.type: DatePicker.Settings.Settings -> Maybe.Maybe ( DatePicker.Utilities.PickerDay, Time.Posix ) -> Maybe.Maybe ( DatePicker.Utilities.PickerDay, Time.Posix ) -> DatePicker.Settings.PresetRangeConfig -> Basics.Bool

values.name: isTimePickerVisible
values.comment: Determines whether the time picker is visible based on the `TimePickerVisibility`.

name: DatePicker.Theme
comment:
This module defines the `Theme` type and a `defaultTheme` for a date picker component.

@docs Theme, defaultTheme

aliases.name: Theme
aliases.comment: The type facilitating the Theme with the most important design tokens
aliases.type: { fontSize : { base : Basics.Float, sm : Basics.Float, xs : Basics.Float, xxs : Basics.Float }, color : { text : { primary : Css.Color, secondary : Css.Color, disabled : Css.Color, error : Css.Color }, primary : { main : Css.Color, contrastText : Css.Color, light : Css.Color }, background : { container : Css.Color, footer : Css.Color, presets : Css.Color, input : Css.Color }, action : { hover : Css.Color }, border : Css.Color }, size : { presetsContainer : Basics.Float, day : Basics.Float, iconButton : Basics.Float, inputElement : Basics.Float }, spacing : { base : Basics.Float }, borderWidth : Basics.Float, borderRadius : { base : Basics.Float, lg : Basics.Float }, boxShadow : { offsetX : Basics.Float, offsetY : Basics.Float, blurRadius : Basics.Float, spreadRadius : Basics.Float, color : Css.Color }, zIndex : Basics.Int, transition : { duration : Basics.Float }, classNamePrefix : String.String }

values.name: defaultTheme
values.comment: The default theme that is included in the defaultSettings

name: DurationDatePicker

unions.name: DatePicker
unions.comment: The opaque type representing a particular date picker instance.

unions.name: Msg
unions.comment: Internal Msg's to update the picker.

values.name: closePicker
values.comment: Close the provided date picker and receive the updated picker instance.

values.name: hasError
values.comment: Indicates whether the DatePicker's date inputs have a validation error

values.name: init
values.comment: Instantiates and returns a date picker.

values.name: isOpen
values.comment: Indicates whether the DatePicker is open

values.name: openPicker
values.comment:
Open the provided date picker and receive the updated picker instance. Also
takes a default time the picker should center on (in the event a time has not yet
been picked) as well as the picked start and end times. A common example of a default time
would be the datetime for the current day.
values.type: String.String -> DatePicker.Settings.Settings -> Time.Posix -> Maybe.Maybe Time.Posix -> Maybe.Maybe Time.Posix -> DurationDatePicker.DatePicker msg -> ( DurationDatePicker.DatePicker msg, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg )

values.name: subscriptions
values.comment: Events external to the picker to which it is subscribed.

values.name: update
values.comment:
Update the DurationDatePicker according to the given internal msg.

Returns the updated picker and the currently selected start and end datetime, if available.
values.type: DatePicker.Settings.Settings -> DurationDatePicker.Msg -> DurationDatePicker.DatePicker msg -> ( ( DurationDatePicker.DatePicker msg, Maybe.Maybe ( Time.Posix, Time.Posix ) ), Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg )

values.name: updatePickerPosition
values.comment:
Returns the command to update the trigger & picker DOM elements' instances.
Is used internally but can also be used externally in case of a changing viewport
(e.g. onScroll or onResize).
values.type: DurationDatePicker.DatePicker msg -> ( DurationDatePicker.DatePicker msg, Platform.Cmd.Cmd msg )

values.name: view
values.comment:
The date picker view. Simply pass it the configured settings
and the date picker instance you wish to view.

values.name: viewDurationInput
values.comment:
The duration date inputs view with the date picker opening on click.
Pass it the configured settings, the base time, the picked start and end times
and the date picker instance you wish to view.
values.type: List.List (Html.Attribute msg) -> DatePicker.Settings.Settings -> Time.Posix -> Maybe.Maybe Time.Posix -> Maybe.Maybe Time.Posix -> DurationDatePicker.DatePicker msg -> Html.Html msg
